title: Cotoneaster robustior
intro: Cotoneaster robustior, also known as the large-leaved cotoneaster, is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia, belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cotoneaster robustior include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions like bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Cotoneaster robustior is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is native to the Himalayas and parts of China. The plant is a deciduous shrub with oval-shaped leaves and white flowers that are followed by red berries. Cotoneaster robustior is often used as an ornamental plant due to its attractive foliage and fruit. It is classified as a member of the genus Cotoneaster, which includes various other species of shrubs and small trees.
uses: Cotoneaster robustior. Unfortunately, there is limited information available on the medicinal uses of Cotoneaster robustior.
